articles.title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce in NYC ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
articles.summary: The star-studded celebration saw several streets in Midtown Manhattan shut, as hordes of fans gathered outside the arena.

articles.title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
articles.summary: More than 165 million people are enduring dangerous heat and humidity, with temperatures expected to stay at 38C (100F) for several days.

articles.title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
articles.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

articles.title: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
articles.summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

articles.title: Anguished families left to identify Venezuela quake victims at makeshift morgue
articles.summary: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

articles.title: 'Flamingo Revolution' takes off as thousands demand Albanian PM's resignation
articles.summary: Flamingos have become a national symbol of protest, triggered by opposition to a luxury development.

articles.title: Pope urges Europe to do more for migrants while visiting gateway island
articles.summary: The pontiff pays tribute to migrants who lost their lives at sea at a cemetery on the Italian island of Lampedusa.

articles.title: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
articles.summary: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out the scene of the attack.

articles.title: France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather
articles.summary: Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.

articles.title: Row after Indian state drops eggs from school lunch menu
articles.summary: West Bengal's move to replace eggs in some school meals has sparked a wider debate over nutrition and choice.
